device_name: OPTIS Mobile Next Imaging System, OPTIS Integrated Next Imaging System
intended_use: The OPTIS Next Imaging System with a compatible Dragonfly OPTIS Imaging Catheter or Dragonfly OpStar Imaging Catheter is intended for the imaging of coronary arteries and is indicated in patients who
intended_use_full: The OPTIS Next Imaging System with a compatible Dragonfly OPTIS Imaging Catheter or Dragonfly OpStar Imaging Catheter is intended for the imaging of coronary arteries and is indicated in patients who are candidates for transluminal interventional procedures. The system is intended for use in the catheterization and related cardiovascular specialty laboratories and will further compute and display various physiological parameters based on the output from one or more electrodes, transducers, or measuring devices. The physician may use the acquired physiological parameters, along with knowledge of patient history, medical expertise, and clinical judgment to determine if therapeutic intervention is indicated.
target_condition: coronary artery stenosis/lesions requiring hemodynamic assessment for potential transluminal interventional procedures
ground_truth: Clinical outcomes from published peer-reviewed studies validating RFR against other non-hyperemic pressure ratios and FFR
other_metrics: RFR cut-off of 0.89 for hemodynamic significance (validated in over 2,500 patients across published studies); RFR ≤ 0.89 indicates hemodynamically significant lesion; RFR > 0.89 indicates non-hemodynamically significant lesion
